title: tanacetum djilgense
intro: Tanacetum djilgense, also known as Dorrigo purslane, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Dorrigo Plateau of New South Wales, Australia. It is a succulent herb that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Tanacetum djilgense are attributed to its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat wounds and skin conditions. In terms of herbal preparations, Tanacetum djilgense can be consumed as a tea, applied topically as a salve or oil, or used in a capsule or tablet form, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to its limited research and potential interactions with other medications.
